QF.id: Issue Management - General Obligations and Due Diligence_18

question.text: Under the Companies Rules, audited financial statements included in a prospectus must not be more than ______ old from the date of the prospectus.

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** As per the Companies Rules (made under the Companies Act), audited financial statements included in a prospectus must not be more than 120 days old from the date of the prospectus. This is a separate and additional requirement to the SEBI ICDR 6-month rule.
**The Trap:** "6 months" is the SEBI ICDR requirement (not the Companies Act requirement), and "180 days" is the numerical equivalent of 6 months — both relate to a different regulation. The Companies Act imposes a stricter 120-day standard. Candidates must know which rule belongs to which legislation.
**Cross-Exam Context:** NISM exams test the interplay between SEBI regulations and the Companies Act. Understanding which requirement comes from which law — and which is stricter — is a recurring theme.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Since 120 days is stricter than 6 months, in practice the Companies Act rule will often be the binding constraint. Always check the prospectus date against the audit report date — if it is more than 120 days, you need updated audited financials.
